steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers claim endothermy enabled mosasaurs to live in cold polar waters. Supporting evidence must directly link mosasaur presence in polar regions to their physiology — the decisive clue is the contrast between endothermic mosasaurs and nonendothermic reptiles in those same locations.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘body temperatures easier to determine from mosasaur enamel’: this is about research methodology, not habitat range; it says nothing about where mosasaurs actually lived. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘equal fossils of mosasaurs and nonendothermic reptiles near poles’: roughly equal numbers would suggest endothermy gave no advantage in polar waters, which weakens the claim rather than supporting it. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘mosasaur fossils common near poles; nonendothermic reptile fossils rare there’: this directly supports the claim — mosasaurs inhabited polar waters while nonendothermic reptiles largely did not, suggesting endothermy was the enabling factor. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Late Cretaceous polar seawater was warmer than today’: warmer-than-today water could still be cold enough to matter, so this finding neither confirms nor denies that endothermy was needed to survive there. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
